capability.name: Performance Diagnosis
capability.description: Identify and fix performance bottlenecks from descriptions, metrics, and code patterns. Distinguish between network, rendering, memory, and algorithmic performance issues. Know which tool to reach for and which metric matters.

sets.symptom: The page takes 8 seconds to become interactive after loading. The HTML appears quickly but nothing is clickable for several seconds.
sets.context: A React SPA with 45 npm dependencies. No code splitting. The main bundle is 2.3MB gzipped. The page has 12 components that all render on initial load.
sets.metrics:
- TTFB: 180ms
- FCP: 900ms
- LCP: 1.2s
- TTI: 8.1s
- Total JS: 2.3MB gzipped (~7MB parsed)
- Main thread blocking time: 6.2s

sets.symptom: Scrolling through a list of 500 items is janky — frames drop to 10-15fps and the UI stutters visibly.
sets.context: A React app rendering a list of cards. Each card has an image, text, and a 'like' button. No virtualization. Each card has its own useEffect that sets up an IntersectionObserver. The parent list re-renders on every scroll event to update a scroll position indicator.
sets.metrics:
- 500 DOM nodes for list items
- 500 IntersectionObserver instances
- scroll event fires ~60 times/sec
- Each scroll event triggers parent re-render
- React DevTools shows 500 component re-renders per scroll

sets.symptom: The dashboard API endpoint takes 4.5 seconds to respond. It was 200ms a month ago.
sets.context: A Node.js/Express API. The dashboard endpoint aggregates data from 3 database queries. One month ago a new 'activity feed' feature was added that queries a large table. The table has 2M rows and grows by 50k/day.
sets.metrics:
- Overall response: 4.5s
- Query 1 (projects): 45ms
- Query 2 (tasks): 120ms
- Query 3 (activity feed): 4.2s
- Activity table: 2M rows, no index on created_at or user_id
- EXPLAIN shows sequential scan on activity table

criteria.description: Proposed relevant investigation steps with specific tools
Defined how to verify the fix with specific metrics

sets.optimization_target: Re-rendering of a settings page component that takes 3ms to render
sets.context: An internal admin tool used by 5 people. The settings page is visited maybe twice a day per user. The developer wants to add React.memo to every component and useMemo to every derived value.
sets.current_performance: Render time: 3ms. Page load: 200ms. No user complaints.

sets.optimization_target: A product listing page that takes 6 seconds to load on mobile devices with 3G connections
sets.context: An e-commerce site. 60% of traffic is mobile. Conversion rate on mobile is 1.2% vs 3.8% on desktop. The page loads 4MB of images and 1.5MB of JS before showing any products.
sets.current_performance: LCP: 6.1s on 3G, 1.8s on fast connection. Bounce rate on mobile: 62%. Revenue impact estimated at $40k/month.

criteria.description: Correctly assessed whether optimization is warranted
Analyzed the cost of optimization vs. benefit
Redirected attention appropriately (to actual problems or specific optimizations)
